message:
feat(analytics): make chart data loading async to prevent UI blocking

The analytics chart data load was previously synchronous — calling
load_chart_data() directly on the main thread inside the
AnalyticsLoadRequested handler. For large databases this could freeze the
TUI for several seconds while SQLite queries ran.

This commit refactors the load path to be fully asynchronous:

- Add LoadingContext::StateLoad variant for granular loading state tracking
- Add CassMsg::AnalyticsChartDataLoaded and AnalyticsChartDataFailed
  message variants to handle the async result lifecycle
- AnalyticsLoadRequested now spawns a background task via ftui::Cmd::task
  that opens a separate read-only SQLite connection, runs the chart data
  queries off the main thread, and dispatches the result back as a message
- On success, AnalyticsChartDataLoaded populates the cache and clears the
  loading spinner; on failure, AnalyticsChartDataFailed sets a status
  message so the user sees what went wrong
- Test builds get a fast path that returns AnalyticsChartData::default()
  immediately to avoid needing a real database in unit tests

Also updates .beads issue tracker state (B.2.1 now in_progress).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
